definition:
1. dimly seen, dim, faint, baffling sight , εἴδωλον ἀμ. a shadowy  spectre, (Odyssey by Homer) 
2. having no light, darksome , νύξ (Lucian):—; blind, sightless , (Sophocles Tragicus); so, ἀμαυρῶι κώλωι with blind  foot, said of a blind man, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
3. metaphorically, 
4. dim, obscure, uncertain , κληδών (Aeschulus Tragicus); ἐλπίς (Plutarch) 
5. obscure, unknown , (Hesiod) 
6. gloomy, troubled , φρήν (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
7. active enfeebling , νοῦσος [variant datesAnthology Palantina (ML)
